---
title: "About This Website"
---

My portfolio shows my growth as a data science student over the semester. Across these projects, I practiced moving from messy or unfamiliar data toward clearer questions, cleaner datasets, and more meaningful visualizations. I worked with several kinds of data, including mobile game analytics, reading attention patterns, Spotify audio features, mental health workforce data, and eye-tracking data from my own research.

A major goal of my portfolio was to strengthen my data managing and analytic skills. Some projects were more playful, like exploring Spotify tracks and mobile game data, while others connected more directly to my academic and research interests, such as mental health access and eye-tracking. Together, these pieces helped me practice making data useful, understandable, and connected to real questions about behavior, attention, access, and user experience. This portfolio also reflects the kind of work I want to keep developing for my thesis and beyond.
